name: national_high
_dataset_notes: 15 teachers, 4 classrooms each, 25 students = 1,500 students total.
Each teacher still has 100 students (same per-teacher scale as national_medium).
More teachers provide more ingest target variety and represent the broader teacher pool.
Hot data: 1,500 students x 2 hot weeks x 3 runs x ~20 turns ≈ 180,000 hot turn events (T=20 per docs/research/ingest-capacity-model.md).
Dataset prep will take 15-30 minutes; ~540,000 cold turns to compact.
At the corrected steady-state ingest rate this scenario sits near the measured hardware ceiling on 2 vCPU; the bell-burst is a separate regime.
_traffic_notes: Derived from the corrected national load model in docs/research/ingest-capacity-model.md (high adoption: 75% of 60,000 grade 4-6 pupils).
Steady-state CCU peak at high adoption: ~6,365 students mid-run nationally.
Ingest RPS (steady): CCU / R = 6,365 / 400 ≈ 16 ingest/s (R = T × w = 20 × 20s = 400s mean run length).
Per-student rate λ = 1/R ≈ 0.0025 ingest/s — 1 POST /panel/api/runs/ingest/ per completed Run (no heartbeats).
The lesson-bell burst (~44 RPS for 60s) is a separate regime modeled by the lesson_bell scenario.
Teacher traffic: ~120 concurrent teachers at high scale; dashboard ~3 RPS, analytics/turn_insights ~2 RPS each.
VUs are sized very generously so drops come from server response time, not VU starvation.
At ingest p95 latency of ~250ms under load: 16 × 0.25 = ~4 steady-state VUs; pre=120 gives ample headroom.
Expected outcome on the optimized 2 vCPU stack: load_health green at steady; the bell-burst regime is where headroom evaluation belongs.
